accept: Test repo exists with .act/ initialized (via 'act init' once available; manually placed for now if needed) and either no CLAUDE.md or a minimal one.
Fresh Claude Code session in that repo successfully uses act_next or 'act ready' / 'act update --claim' without explicit prompting beyond the natural task.
If activation fails, the skill description is iterated until activation succeeds, and the iteration is recorded in the act-evaluation doc.
Comparison to previous CLAUDE.md-only approach: at least equivalent agent quality on a single-issue end-to-end run.
payload.description:
We just promoted CLAUDE.md's canonical workflow to a global skill at ~/.claude/skills/act/SKILL.md (act-256c). Implicit validation: it appears in Claude Code's skill list when discovered. Not yet validated: whether it actually activates when an agent walks into a repo with just .act/ and a CLAUDE.md that defers to the skill.

Concrete test: set up a small test repo (NOT act itself; this repo's slim CLAUDE.md + dogfood is a confounder). Drop .act/ into it. Have a fresh Claude Code session with a generic prompt ('what's next to work on here?' or similar) and observe whether the agent uses the act workflow without being told.

If it activates: validation done; document the trigger pattern for the global skill in the act-evaluation doc.

If it does not activate: tighten the description, add stronger trigger keywords, retest. Compare effectiveness to the previous CLAUDE.md-only approach (where every project carried a copy of the workflow doc) — the bar is 'as good as or better than CLAUDE.md alone'.
payload.title: validate the global act skill activates correctly in a fresh project
